overview: Izzet Prowess is an aggressive Pioneer deck that combines cheap cantrips, burn spells, and efficient threats to apply constant pressure. It leverages Cori-Steel Cutter to generate tokens from spellcast triggers, increasing resilience and damage output. The strategy focuses on explosive early turns and proactive graveyard feeding while using instant-speed interaction to control the pace.
win_conditions: Triggering Cori-Steel Cutter with cheap cantrips and spells to generate 1/1 tokens and deal damage
Explosive turns with Monastery Swiftspear and Slickshot Show-Off to close out games quickly
Using Burst Lightning and Play with Fire to clear blockers and deal direct damage
Leveraging Stormchaser’s Talent for late-game damage and breathing room

keep_criteria: Hands with one-mana spells and early threats like Monastery Swiftspear
Hands with dual lands and cantrips to trigger Cori-Steel Cutter
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (at least one Island and one Mountain), at least one Prowess creature (Cori-Steel Cutter), and 2-3 cheap instants/sorceries (Opt, Shock, Lightning Strike, etc.).
Hands with 1 land and 3+ spells are playable if they contain a Prowess creature and a cantrip.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking cheap spells to trigger equipment
Hands with only high-cost cards or multiple lands
Mulligan if you have 3+ lands with no spells, no Prowess creatures, or only expensive spells with no early game.

examples.reason: Provides an early threat, multiple cheap spells for Cutter triggers, and consistent land drops.

early_game: Play cheap threats and cantrips to trigger Cori-Steel Cutter
Apply pressure with Monastery Swiftspear and burn spells
Use Plot cost on Slickshot Show-Off to cast multiple spells
Turn 1: Play a land, cast a cantrip or cheap instant if available.
Turn 2: Play a second land, cast Cori-Steel Cutter, then follow up with an instant/sorcery to trigger Prowess and attack for 3+ damage.
If drawing lands, play them and hold up removal for potential early threats.
Always cast instants/sorceries before creatures to maximize Prowess scaling, and prioritize keeping a creature on the board to maintain pressure.
mid_game: Filter deck with Play with Fire scry effect at upkeep
Bounce problematic permanents with Into the Flood Maw
Maintain tempo with Spell Pierce and instant-speed cantrips
Balance mana for Prowess triggers versus holding up removal.
Use cantrips to dig for answers or threats without overcommitting.
Avoid casting multiple instants/sorceries in a row if you lack a creature to grow, as this wastes value.
Manage your graveyard carefully if the format includes graveyard hate, but prioritize keeping spells in hand for instant-speed interaction.
Commit threats when you have sufficient instants/sorceries in hand or graveyard to keep growing them, and when the opponent has no sweepers.
Hold back if they have potential board wipes or if you're low on resources.
late_game: Rely on Stormchaser’s Talent for damage and resilience
Use Den of the Bugbear and Otawara, Soaring City for utility and threats
Avoid bad topdecks by sideboarding out Monstrous Rage against heavy removal
Emberheart Challenger and fully grown Cori-Steel Cutters.
Win via combat damage or direct damage from Stormchaser's Talent.
Use cantrips to dig for the right finisher and ensure you have enough instants/sorceries to grow them to lethal size.
Go all-in when you have 3+ instants/sorceries in hand/graveyard, the opponent has no sweepers, and you can attack for lethal or force a trade that leaves you ahead.
Commit when you've forced interaction and can safely grow a threat to 5+ toughness.
Use Stormchaser's Talent to clear blockers or finish the game directly.

key_cards.name: Cori-Steel Cutter
key_cards.notes: Triggers on spellcast to create tokens and deal damage, central to the deck's resilience.

key_cards.name: Monastery Swiftspear
key_cards.notes: Classic red aggro creature that enables explosive turns from turn two.

key_cards.name: Slickshot Show-Off
key_cards.notes: Exponentially grows with one-mana spells and can close games via Plot cost.

key_cards.name: Stormchaser’s Talent
key_cards.notes: Provides a 1/1 Prowess creature and a second ability for late-game breathing room.

key_cards.name: Burst Lightning
key_cards.notes: Handles minor blockers and amplifies creature damage, often used with kicker.

key_cards.name: Play with Fire
key_cards.notes: Deals damage and provides Scry 1 to filter the top card, usable at upkeep.

key_cards.name: Into the Flood Maw
key_cards.notes: Bounces noncreature permanents like Temporary Lockdown or Authority of the Consuls to regain tempo.

key_cards.name: Opt
key_cards.notes: Prioritized for instant-speed interaction to trigger Cori-Steel Cutter on opponent's turn.

key_cards.name: Annul
key_cards.notes: Complements Spell Pierce to handle key spells like Fable of the Mirror-Breaker or Unholy Annex.

key_cards.name: Screaming Nemesis
key_cards.notes: Counters life gain and pressures midrange decks with high damage potential.

sequencing_tips: Cast instant-speed cantrips like Opt and Consider on the opponent's turn to trigger Cori-Steel Cutter before they respond.
Use Play with Fire's Scry 1 effect during your upkeep to filter the top card before drawing.
Hold Into the Flood Maw until the end of the opponent's turn to bounce temporary answers like Temporary Lockdown and attack for lethal.
Common mistake: Overcasting instants/sorceries without threats to grow, or overcommitting to a single Prowess creature.
Always keep a Prowess creature on the board to maximize trigger value.
Remember that Prowess triggers when you cast the spell, not when it resolves, so timing matters.
Use cantrips to dig for both threats and answers.
In specific formats, check for format-specific hate cards like graveyard hate or specific removal.
Practice counting your instants/sorceries in hand and graveyard to accurately predict Prowess scaling.

matchups.advice: Side in Screaming Nemesis to counter life gain and apply pressure. Watch for removal and life total swings.
Use Abrade and Redcap Melee for creature removal. Watch for graveyard hate and efficient blockers.
Side in Redcap Melee to handle their aggressive creatures. Watch for early burn and board wipes.
Side in Abrade and Annul to disrupt their combo pieces. Watch for Phoenix recursion and burn spells.
Side out Monstrous Rage to reduce bad topdecks against heavy removal. Watch for counterspells and board wipes.
Side in Unlicensed Hearse and Abrade. Watch for sacrifice outlets and removal.
Side in Abrade and Redcap Melee. Watch for token generation and removal.
Side in Redcap Melee and Abrade to handle Greasefang and other threats. Watch for big creatures and burn.

Use cheap, redundant spells to force counterspells. Cast threats in response to their counterspells. Use cantrips to dig for answers. Don't overcommit to one big play. Value Prowess triggers over raw damage, and be willing to trade a creature if it forces a counter and leaves you ahead in cards.

Apply early pressure to punish their mana development. Use removal on their mana dorks/rocks. Stabilize with cheap instants and close quickly. Don't let them reach their late-game threats; win before they can cast their big spells. Use Stormchaser's Talent to remove key ramp pieces.

Use removal on key early threats. Cast Prowess creatures to block and grow. Don't overextend into combat tricks. Stabilize by turn 3-4. Use cantrips to dig for answers and keep pressure on. Win by outvaluing their early board with efficient removal and growing threats to block their entire lineup.

Pressure early to force interaction. Use cantrips to dig for answers. Win with unblockable Prowess threats or direct damage. Don't let them stabilize with sweepers. Force them to use resources on your early threats, then close with a fully grown Emberheart Challenger. Use Stormchaser's Talent to clear blockers or finish the game.

Value Prowess triggers. Cast instants/sorceries in response to their creatures. Sideboard in more removal/counterspells. Win by outvaluing spell interactions and closing with a bigger threat. Track their graveyard and hand size, and be willing to trade creatures if it leaves you with more resources. Use Stormchaser's Talent to remove their key threats or finish the game.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (e.g., Supreme Verdict, Wrath of God), targeted removal, and ramp/mana rocks.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: mixing cheap cantrips, damage spells, and efficient threats to pressure the opponent while creating new tokens every turn with the equipment

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: easily take advantage of the artifact and trigger its ability every turn
guarantees explosive turns with it from the second turn onwards
deal with minor blockers while amplifying the damage output of our creatures
guarantees more breathing room in the late game to pull out the last points of damage

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: prioritize Opt and Consider to have more instant-speed interactions that allow the artifact to trigger on the opponent's turn
use, for example, at our upkeep to filter the top before drawing
common to use it to remove Temporary Lockdown at the end of the opponent's turn to return our permanents to the board and attack the next turn for lethal

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: remove Temporary Lockdown at the end of the opponent's turn
bounce Authority of the Consuls to return with Slickshot Show-Off
deals with Greasefang, Okiba Boss and the like
format that includes Cori-Steel Cutter, Fable of the Mirror-Breaker, Unholy Annex, Caretaker’s Talent, Artist's Talent and Monument to Endurance
